title: phacelia secunda subsp. plantaginea
intro: Phacelia secunda subsp. plantaginea is a subspecies of flowering plant in the borage family (Boraginaceae) native to California and Oregon in the United States. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can be used to treat wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Phacelia secunda subsp. plantaginea include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory function, and support the immune system, making it a valuable herb in traditional medicine. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve for various herbal remedies, and its leaves and stems can also be used in infusions and decoctions to harness its medicinal properties.
